gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5/high reviewer Pascal found missing has_pending_push_work coverage and missing prefer_cross_layer_blocks delegation, then found the duplicated native-probe contract also needed has_pending_push_work; all findings were fixed and final re-review reported no remaining issues.

python -m pytest -q tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_*.py tests/test_native_probe_factories.py tests/test_adapter_scaffold.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py tests/test_repository_hygiene.py -> 201 passed
GPT-5.5/high reviewer Pascal found optional-hook and duplicate-contract gaps; both were fixed and final re-review reported no remaining issues
what_changed: Extended the vendored vLLM DocumentKVConnector to expose and safely default/delegate the optional vLLM V1 connector lifecycle hooks used by current runtimes.
Delegated provider preferences for cross-layer KV blocks and push-mode pending work while preserving no-provider inert defaults.
Aligned the package vLLM runtime contract and Cachet native-probe contract tables so strict release diagnostics describe the same optional lifecycle surface.
why: The provider-backed g6/L4 launch path should not fail or silently hide provider capabilities when a newer vLLM runtime calls optional KV connector hooks around scheduling, metadata, stats, handshakes, or shutdown.
